content.full_text:
Office of the
CITY MANAGER

FRED AGUIAR
Mayor

RICHARD D. ROWE
City Manager

DIANE J. ERWIN
Mayor Pro Tem

DICK SAWHILL
EUNICE ULLOA
AL YANKEY
Council Members

CITY OF CHINO

September 3, 1991


Ms. Jeanette Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Ms. Turvill:

Thank you for your assistance in sending the City of Chino
information on opinions issued by the FPPC relating to which
consultants should be required to file Statements of Economic
Interest. We have reviewed our current consultant contracts and
most consultants have either met the criteria for filing Statements
or are clearly not required to file. However, we request
clarification on several consultants where it is not obvious
whether they should be required to file Statements. Attached is
the list of consultants still in question. These consultants are
grouped by City Department.

I appreciate your assistance. Please call me at (714) 627-7577,
Extension 402, if you require any additional information prior to
responding.

Sincerely,

MARCIA GODWIN
Management Assistant II

MG:ljt
MG0901L.91

Attachments


13220 Central Avenue, Chino, California 91710
Mailing Address: P.O. Box 667, Chino, California 91708-0667
(714) 627-7577

CITY OF CHINO CONSULTANTS


Clarification is requested for the following list of consultants
regarding filing requirements.

City Manager's Office

1.   Attorney.  Provides legal expertise related to Federal
     Aviation Authority and Department of Airports policies and
     regulations.  The City has been seeking airplane noise
     reduction over the City of Chino caused by airplanes from
     Ontario International Airport.  Attorney has been paid
     approximately $40,000 per year.  Services not expected to
     continue past fiscal year 1991-92.


Community Development (includes Planning, Redevelopment, and
Economic Development)

1.   Community Development currently requires firms to submit
     financial disclosure statements (not Statement of Economic
     Interest) as part of all Requests for Proposal. These RFPs
     include Environmental Impact Reports, Specific Plans, and
     other contracts bid as single, specific projects.  Are these
     types of contracts considered single projects with substantial
     intervening review or should Statements of Economic Interest
     be submitted?

2.   Consulting Firm. Prepared study on whether warehouse-type
     companies in the City of Chino were causing a negative or
     positive financial impact. Included analysis of impacts on
     City infrastructure and City services required versus sales
     tax and other revenue.  $17,000 contract, which has been
     completed.

3.   Consulting Firm.  Pursues economic development opportunities
     upon referral of the City.  Firm interfaces with prospective
     new businesses regarding possible financial arrangements and
     the development process.  Ongoing contract.

4.   Property Appraisal Firm. Appraises property that the City may
     be interested in acquiring. Ongoing, payment varies depending
     upon assignment.

5.   Consultant Firm.  Preparing Economic Development Strategy.
     Includes strategy and how to implement.  $61,300 contract, in
     effect until strategy is completed.

6.   Consultant. Provides financial analysis for the Redevelopment
     Agency.  Includes financial advice and advise on strategies.
     Ongoing contract with hourly fees.


[illegible]

CITY OF CHINO CONSULTANTS
PAGE TWO


7.    Sprinkler Review Service.  Performs fire sprinkler plan
      checks.  Ongoing contract.  Paid with percentage of fire
      sprinkler permit fees.

Management Services (includes Finance, Purchasing, and Personnel)

1.    Attorney. Provides information on labor relations, including
      recommendations about possible disciplinary actions. Ongoing
      contract.

2.    Financial consultant. Prepares arbitrage calculations. Also
      answers questions related to assessment districts.  Would
      coordinate foreclosures, if any occur in the future.  $25,000
      per year.

3.    Financial consultant. Prepares reports on sales tax revenues,
      including top sales tax producers and sales tax revenues by
      type of business. Apparently makes some recommendations about
      ways to increase sales tax revenues. Ongoing contract.

Public Works

1.    Inspection Services.  General land development related
      inspection services.  Purchase order of $1,000.

2.    Engineering Consultant.  General engineering services.
      Approximately $7,620.

3.    Engineering Plan Checking Consultants. Two consultants hired
      to perform engineering plan checking services.  Plan checking
      had been performed in-house and is now contracted.  Two
      contracts, one $10,000 and one $9,675.  Similar contracts can
      be expected in the future.

4.    Traffic consultant. Provides traffic consulting services in
      support of traffic engineering section.  Ongoing contract,
      approximately $72,265 annually.

MG:ljt
MG0901L.91

sections.question_synthetic: The City of Chino requests FPPC clarification on whether various consultants under contract with the city should be required to file Statements of Economic Interest, particularly for consultants in Community Development, Management Services, and Public Works departments where filing requirements are unclear.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City of Chino to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal Question or Conclusion sections present. Document contains detailed consultant descriptions organized by department. This appears to be seeking FPPC guidance rather than a response to such guidance.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The City of Chino requests FPPC clarification on whether various consultants under contract with the city should be required to file Statements of Economic Interest, particularly for consultants in Community Development, Management Services, and Public Works departments where filing requirements are unclear.

embedding.summary: The City of Chino writes to the FPPC requesting clarification on filing requirements for Statements of Economic Interest for multiple consultants across various city departments, providing details on 14 specific consultant contracts to help determine which ones should be required to file.
